title: Chaotic Friends
theme: One harmless hang turns into group liability.

modifiers.label: Main Character Energy
modifiers.description: The room starts making decisions like the crowd is already chanting their names.

modifiers.label: Viral Momentum
modifiers.description: Everything gets louder because the internet has started paying attention.

modifiers.label: Mistaken Legitimacy
modifiers.description: Enough strangers believe the lie that doors start opening anyway.

modifiers.label: Cashflow Delusion
modifiers.description: Revenue appears and the room immediately treats itself like a real business.

nodes.prompt: Your group finds an unattended karaoke machine outside a convenience store.
nodes.resultText: {{spotlight}} gets pushed to the center of the mess and the room picks the exact level of public embarrassment to pin on them.

socialPrompt.prompt: Who in this room would make this karaoke situation worse?
socialPrompt.voteIntro: The room picked {{spotlight}}. Decide how to weaponize that.
socialPrompt.receiptTemplate: {{count}} players said {{spotlight}} would turn this into a public liability.

choices.label: Make {{spotlight}} steal the mic
choices.resultText: {{spotlight}} lunges for instant karaoke authority and turns a sidewalk into a stage.

choices.label: Make {{spotlight}} sell tickets
choices.resultText: {{spotlight}} appoints themselves finance and starts charging strangers for the chaos.

choices.label: Make {{spotlight}} pretend it's art
choices.resultText: {{spotlight}} rebrands trespassing as an immersive installation with dangerous confidence.

nodes.prompt: Three strangers demand that {{spotlight}} deliver the group's best breakup anthem right now.
nodes.resultText: The room escalates from cringe to commitment and leaves {{spotlight}} holding the mic.

choices.label: Tell {{spotlight}} to scream-sing with conviction
choices.resultText: {{spotlight}} turns the anthem into a public emergency and somehow boosts attendance.

choices.label: Make {{spotlight}} turn it into a crowd singalong
choices.resultText: {{spotlight}} hands strangers the chorus and accidentally discovers momentum.

nodes.prompt: Someone actually pays and asks {{spotlight}} if this is a legal event.
nodes.resultText: The room decides whether {{spotlight}} should lie with confidence or monetize the panic.

roundTemplate.privatePrompt: Who in this room should handle this because they'd sell the lie best?
roundTemplate.voteIntro: The room thinks {{spotlight}} can carry this. Decide how they try it.
roundTemplate.receiptTemplate: {{count}} players privately said {{spotlight}} could bluff this room.

choices.label: Have {{spotlight}} say yes confidently
choices.resultText: {{spotlight}} sells a fake permit vibe before anyone can challenge it.

choices.label: Have {{spotlight}} open a merch tab immediately
choices.resultText: {{spotlight}} pivots from event planning to reckless retail in under ten seconds.

nodes.prompt: A local influencer starts filming while {{spotlight}} tries to defend your performance installation.
nodes.resultText: The room picks the flavor of viral regret and leaves {{spotlight}} on camera.

choices.label: Make {{spotlight}} lean into the bit
choices.resultText: {{spotlight}} turns the clip into a movement before anyone can stop it.

choices.label: Make {{spotlight}} demand sponsorship on camera
choices.resultText: {{spotlight}} sees a ring light and starts negotiating like a failing startup.

nodes.prompt: A borrowed fog cannon arrives and the crowd starts chanting for an encore.

promptVariants.text: A borrowed fog cannon arrives and the crowd starts chanting your names like this was always the plan.

nodes.resultText: The room has to decide how aggressively to mistake applause for infrastructure.

choices.label: Book a rooftop set
choices.resultText: The room upgrades from sidewalk chaos to skyline liability.

choices.label: Stage-dive into trust
choices.resultText: The room lets gravity handle brand strategy.

nodes.prompt: A rival busker demands a dance battle for custody of the karaoke machine.
nodes.resultText: The room weaponizes rhythm or petty legal theory.

roundTemplate.privatePrompt: Who in this room would absolutely overcommit to this dance battle?
roundTemplate.voteIntro: The room thinks {{spotlight}} can sell this showdown. Decide how they try.
roundTemplate.receiptTemplate: {{count}} players privately said {{spotlight}} would escalate this custody battle best.

choices.label: Battle for ownership
choices.resultText: The room treats public humiliation like binding contract law.

choices.label: Make the rival an opener
choices.resultText: The room rebrands a feud as collaborative curation.

nodes.prompt: A city blogger asks for the origin story of your 'festival collective.'

promptVariants.text: A city blogger asks for the origin story of your 'award-winning pop-up festival collective.'

nodes.resultText: The room can either invent lore or hand the mic to bureaucracy.

choices.label: Invent a founding myth
choices.resultText: The room creates oral history for an organization that existed twelve minutes ago.

choices.label: Promise civic enrichment
choices.resultText: You accidentally say enough nonprofit words that people start nodding.

nodes.prompt: The merch table is suddenly out-earning the actual music.
nodes.resultText: The room must decide whether this is a brand pivot or a tax crime.

roundTemplate.privatePrompt: Privately, what would you actually do with this momentum?
roundTemplate.voteIntro: Privately, the room leaned {{option}}. Now decide what the whole group commits to.
roundTemplate.receiptTemplate: {{count}} players privately chose {{option}} before the room locked in a public move.
roundTemplate.distributionIntro: {{count}} private votes landed on {{option}} before the room had to act.

choices.label: Launch VIP lanyards
choices.resultText: The room monetizes nothing with startling professionalism.

choices.label: Offer equity to strangers
choices.resultText: The room invents a cap table in a parking lot and hopes nobody asks follow-up questions.

nodes.prompt: A local brand wants your next stunt to be 'reckless, but family-safe.'
nodes.resultText: The room chooses between a fake corporate pivot and an actual public hazard.

choices.label: Pitch a car wash rooftop set
choices.resultText: The room confuses spectacle with structural safety.

choices.label: Promise a wholesome afterparty
choices.resultText: You sell a lie so ambitious it becomes a logistics problem.

nodes.prompt: Fans demand afterparty coordinates and assume you already rented a venue.
nodes.resultText: The room scrambles to turn confidence into a location.

roundTemplate.privatePrompt: Choose the hidden agenda you want to push before everyone votes.
roundTemplate.voteIntro: Hidden agendas are locked. Decide which lie the room actually commits to.
roundTemplate.receiptTemplate: {{count}} players secretly pushed {{option}} before the room voted in public.
roundTemplate.distributionIntro: {{count}} players quietly pushed {{option}} before the room had to go public.

choices.label: Hijack a community radio station
choices.resultText: The room mistakes emergency access for a booking arrangement.

choices.label: Build a fake VIP queue
choices.resultText: Scarcity appears out of nowhere and people line up to experience it.

nodes.prompt: The rooftop set is one extension cord away from becoming city folklore.

promptVariants.text: The rooftop set is trending hard enough that strangers are climbing fire escapes just to say they were there.

nodes.resultText: The room chooses between legacy and immediate legal consequences.

roundTemplate.privatePrompt: Who in this room should take the fall if the rooftop set gets shut down?
roundTemplate.voteIntro: The room picked {{spotlight}}. Decide exactly how brutally to commit them to this rooftop lie.
roundTemplate.receiptTemplate: {{count}} players quietly pushed {{spotlight}} toward the rooftop fallout.

choices.label: Play until sunrise
choices.resultText: The room commits to myth-making with zero exit strategy.

choices.label: Declare a residency
choices.resultText: You accidentally create a recurring obligation that city officials can now document.

nodes.prompt: A community radio host offers you a live slot if you can sound 'locally beloved.'
nodes.resultText: The room chooses whether to fake sincerity or fake ownership of public infrastructure.

choices.label: Deliver an emotional thank-you speech
choices.resultText: The room accidentally gives the city a lovable underdog story.

choices.label: Announce a citywide tour
choices.resultText: The room schedules events in neighborhoods it has not visited.

nodes.prompt: The fake VIP line is now longer than the actual audience.

promptVariants.text: The fake VIP line is now profitable enough that your least qualified friend starts talking about expansion.

nodes.resultText: The room must decide whether to cash out or escalate the lie.

choices.label: Sell lifetime memberships
choices.resultText: The room invents premium access to a thing it still does not control.

choices.label: Open the barrier and call it community
choices.resultText: You turn scarcity into vibes and somehow get applause for it.

nodes.prompt: A local council meeting mistakes your crew for an arts initiative and invites you to speak.
nodes.resultText: The room either secures funding or secures consequences.

roundTemplate.privatePrompt: Privately, what would you actually do once city officials buy the bit?
roundTemplate.voteIntro: Privately, the room leaned {{option}}. Now decide what the whole group says out loud.
roundTemplate.receiptTemplate: {{count}} players privately picked {{option}} before the room made it civic policy.
roundTemplate.distributionIntro: {{count}} private reads clustered around {{option}} before anyone grabbed the mic.

choices.label: Pitch youth outreach through karaoke
choices.resultText: The room packages chaos as civic healing and watches half the room believe it.

choices.label: Demand a permit on the spot
choices.resultText: The room takes one step too close to actual paperwork and the whole fantasy starts sweating.

nodes.prompt: The room stalls so hard that a city hall intern mistakes your confusion for improvisational theater and hands you an emergency parade permit.
nodes.resultText: Chaos intervenes by making the night official in the worst possible way.

choices.label: Take the permit and reroute the crowd
choices.resultText: The room weaponizes accidental paperwork into a moving VIP disaster.

choices.label: Use it to force a radio hit
choices.resultText: You translate bureaucratic confusion directly into airtime.

nodes.prompt: A fireworks vendor decides your rooftop nonsense is exactly the kind of energy their brand needs.
nodes.resultText: The room acquires pyrotechnics it absolutely did not order.

choices.label: Accept the sponsorship
choices.resultText: The skyline becomes part of the set whether anyone approved it or not.

choices.label: Trade fireworks for radio buzz
choices.resultText: The room barters spectacle into legitimacy with dangerous efficiency.

nodes.prompt: A marching band on break mistakes your event for their call time and starts tuning up beside the merch table.
nodes.resultText: The room is forced to upgrade from scam to parade.

choices.label: Absorb them into the lineup
choices.resultText: The room acquires brass and confidence at the exact same moment.

choices.label: Sell them VIP wristbands
choices.resultText: You monetize confusion so efficiently it starts to resemble strategy.

nodes.prompt: By sunrise, half the city swears your illegal rooftop set was the best music festival they've ever attended.
You end the night with three cease-and-desists, a waitlist, and one deeply regrettable residency contract.
A local paper calls you 'community icons' after witnessing the least organized act of urban bonding on record.
The city awards your fake collective a micro-grant and nobody in the group is mature enough to spend it legally.
A mall security task force shuts the whole operation down and still leaves wearing your merch.
